title: Comparatives
subtitle: Key adjective vocabulary and comparing things

hook.text: Comparatives let you express preference, evaluation, and comparison — essential for real conversation. 'This coffee is better.' 'That's more expensive.' 'This lesson is harder than that one.' Russian comparatives are simple: one form, no agreement needed. You just add чем (than) for the comparison.

why.text: The simple comparative in Russian (лучше, хуже, больше) is invariable — it doesn't change for gender or case. This makes it genuinely easy compared to full adjective agreement. The main thing to know: the comparative is a separate word from the adjective, many are irregular, and comparison uses чем + nominative.

cards.title: Forming simple comparatives
cards.body: Many adjectives form comparatives by replacing the ending with -ЕЕ or -Е. Regular pattern: adjective stem + -ЕЕ. But the most common comparatives are irregular and must be learned as separate words.

cards.title: Key irregular comparatives
cards.body: Learn these first — they're the most frequent comparatives you'll encounter.

cards.title: Making comparisons — чем
cards.body: To say 'A is [comparative] than B', use: A [comparative] чем B. Both A and B stay in nominative. Alternatively: A [comparative] + B in genitive (no чем). The чем construction is easier and less ambiguous.

examples.meaning: This lesson is harder than that one.
I am older than you. (genitive alternative: тебя)

questions.prompt: What is the comparative of хороший?
questions.explanation: хороший (good) → лучше (better). Irregular — must be memorised.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'coffee is better than tea'?
questions.explanation: Comparative + чем + nominative. Кофе лучше, чем чай.

questions.prompt: What is the comparative of трудный (difficult)?

cards.title: Superlative — самый + adjective
cards.body: The superlative (the most X) is formed with самый + adjective in full agreement. самый agrees in gender, number, and case like any adjective. самый лучший = the very best (often doubled with irregular form).

examples.meaning: the biggest city (самый + adj. agree: masculine)

cards.title: Key adjective vocabulary — a practical set
cards.body: These adjectives appear constantly in everyday Russian. Learn them with their masculine form (dictionary form) and the comparative where it's irregular.

questions.prompt: What is the comparative of большой?
How do you say 'this lesson is more interesting'?
She is younger than he: Она ___ него.
questions.explanation: моложе and младше are both comparatives of молодой. него = genitive of он (comparative without чем).
Superlative: самый + adjective (both agree in masculine). самый большой город.

questions.prompt: What is the comparative of плохой (bad)?
Do comparatives agree in gender and number?
questions.explanation: Simple comparatives (лучше, хуже, больше, etc.) are invariable — one form for all genders and numbers.

rules: Key irregular comparatives: лучше (better), хуже (worse), больше (more/bigger), меньше (less/smaller)
Simple comparatives are invariable — no gender or case agreement
Comparison: A [comparative] чем B (both nominative) or A [comparative] + B in genitive
Superlative: самый + full adjective (самый большой, самая красивая)
summary.nextLesson: Next: Block 3 — Everyday language. Unit 7: family, people, and descriptions.

What is the comparative of трудный?
